# Brief: migrate build to PATH-resolved toolchain — generic, multi-agent

**Owners:** any `dc*` agent whose `scripts/build.sh` or `justfile` still uses `cor24-run --run`, `cor24-run --assemble`, or hardcoded `$HOME/github/...` sibling paths.
**Branch (each agent uses their own):** `pr/migrate-toolchain`
**Repos likely affected:** `sw-cor24-{basic, pascal, ocaml, prolog, rpg-ii, forth, macrolisp, apl, monitor, script, pcode, snobol4}` and possibly more — check via the audit step below.

## Context

The COR24 toolchain has consolidated onto a clean PATH-resolved model:

| Tool | What it does |
|---|---|
| `tc24r` | C → COR24 `.s` |
| `cor24-asm` | `.s` → `.lgo` / `.bin` / `.lst` (with optional `--base-addr`) |
| `cor24-emu` | run `.lgo`; load raw bytes via `--load-binary <file>@<addr> --entry <addr>` |
| `cor24-dbg` | debug `.lgo` |
| `link24`, `meta-gen` | PL/SW separate-compilation linker tools |
| `pl-sw` | run the PL/SW compiler (wraps `cor24-emu --lgo plsw.lgo`) |
| `snobol4` | run the SNOBOL4 interpreter (after dcsno's saga ships) |

All of these resolve from `/disk1/github/softwarewrighter/devgroup/work/bin/` on every `dc*`/`dw*` user's PATH. Your build scripts should call them by short name (`pl-sw`, not `$HOME/.../plsw.s` invoked through `cor24-run --run`).

The deprecated `cor24-run` binary still exists for transition but **all flags it provided beyond `--lgo`/`--load-binary`/`--demo` (i.e., `--run`, `--assemble`) have been removed from the new `cor24-emu`**. dcemu's `pr/remove-internal-assembler` saga shipped this. Your scripts using `cor24-run --run` or `--assemble` are running against a stale binary that's slated for retirement; they'll break the moment mike retires `cor24-run` from `work/bin/`.

## Pre-flight: does this apply to you?

```bash
cd ~/github/sw-embed/<your-repo>
grep -rnE 'cor24-run --(run|assemble)|\$HOME/github|\$PLSW_DIR' \
  scripts/ justfile Makefile *.sh 2>/dev/null
```

Any hits → this brief is for you. If clean → you're already migrated, skip.

## Migration mapping (definitive)

| Old | New |
|---|---|
| `cor24-run --run prog.s [opts]` | `cor24-asm prog.s -o prog.lgo && cor24-emu --lgo prog.lgo [opts]` |
| `cor24-run --assemble in.s out.bin out.lst` | `cor24-asm in.s --bin out.bin --listing out.lst` |
| `cor24-run --assemble in.s out.bin /dev/null` | `cor24-asm in.s --bin out.bin` |
| `cor24-run --assemble in.s out.bin /dev/null --base-addr 0x1000` | `cor24-asm in.s --bin out.bin --base-addr 0x1000` |
| `cor24-run --load-binary file@addr --entry addr` | `cor24-emu --load-binary file@addr --entry addr` (same flag spelling) |
| `cor24-run --terminal` (with `--lgo`/`--load-binary`) | `cor24-emu --terminal` (same) |
| `$HOME/github/sw-embed/sw-cor24-plsw/build/plsw.s` (and feeding to cor24-run --run) | `pl-sw` (already wraps the equivalent run-the-compiler dance) |
| `$HOME/.../sw-cor24-plsw/components/linker/target/release/link24` | just `link24` (on PATH) |
| `$HOME/.../sw-cor24-plsw/components/linker/target/release/meta-gen` | just `meta-gen` (on PATH) |
| `$HOME/github/sw-embed/sw-cor24-pcode/target/release/pl24r` | (still per-repo for now) `../sw-cor24-pcode/target/release/pl24r` (relative, not `$HOME`) — or wait until pl24r/pa24r are also installed to `work/bin/` (mike-side TODO) |

## Common patterns

### Compiling `.plsw` source (the PL/SW pipeline)

dcpls migrated their `scripts/pipeline.sh` — copy the pattern:

```bash
# Build INPUT via FILE:/SOURCE: protocol
INPUT=$(printf 'c\nFILE:%s\n%s\n\x1ESOURCE:\n%s\n\x04' \
    "$(basename "$MACRO_FILE")" "$(cat "$MACRO_FILE")" "$(cat "$MAIN_PLSW")")

# Compile via pl-sw (NOT cor24-run --run)
COMPILER_OUT=$(pl-sw -u "$INPUT" --speed 0 -n 200_000_000 -t 600 2>&1)

# Extract assembly markers (per-repo specific)
echo "$COMPILER_OUT" | sed -n '/^--- generated assembly ---/,/^--- end ---/p' > out.s

# Assemble with the new tool
cor24-asm out.s -o out.lgo
```

### Compiling `.c` source (the tc24r pipeline)

```bash
tc24r src/foo.c -o build/foo.s -I include   # produces COR24 .s
cor24-asm build/foo.s -o build/foo.lgo      # assembles to .lgo
cor24-emu --lgo build/foo.lgo               # runs
```

### Linker tests (with `--base-addr`)

Pass-2 reassembly now works:
```bash
# was: cor24-run --assemble mod.s mod.bin mod.lst --base-addr 0x100
cor24-asm mod.s --bin mod.bin --listing mod.lst --base-addr 0x100
```

## Robustness fixes worth folding in

While migrating, also fix these common issues if your repo has them:

1. **`build/` doesn't auto-create.** If `tc24r src/main.c -o build/foo.s` fails on a fresh clone with "cannot write build/foo.s: No such file or directory", add `mkdir -p build` to your `build:` recipe.
2. **`$HOME/...` paths assume a specific layout** that doesn't hold for d* users in the multi-user devgroup setup. Replace with sibling-relative or PATH-resolved.
3. **Mac-specific paths** (`/Users/mike/...`) are bugs; use sibling-relative paths.
4. **`stat -f%z`** (BSD/Mac) doesn't work on Linux; use `wc -c <` or `stat -c%s` instead. dcpls fixed this in their linker tests — see their commit.

## What goes in this PR

1. Replace every `cor24-run --run`/`--assemble` callsite per the mapping above.
2. Replace `$HOME/.../sw-cor24-X/...` sibling-clone paths with PATH-resolved tools where a wrapper exists, or proper sibling-relative paths otherwise.
3. Add `mkdir -p build` (or equivalent) where build artifacts are written.
4. Run your full test suite (whatever your repo has — `just test`, `./scripts/test.sh`, demos, etc.) and verify it passes against the new toolchain.
5. Update `README.md` setup instructions if they reference the old workflow (sibling clone of plsw, etc.) — point at the new workflow (`pl-sw` on PATH).
6. Single saga commit (or split per-script if cleaner).

## What does NOT go in this PR

- No actual code logic changes — purely build/script migration.
- No new features.
- No reaching into other repos (other than the read-only "sibling clone for cargo path-deps" pattern).

## When done

Workflow: `dg-new-feature migrate-toolchain` (creates `feat/migrate-toolchain` from `dev`) → implement and verify → `dg-mark-pr` to rename to `pr/migrate-toolchain` when ready. Signal mike. After relay, your build will work for any d* user without hardcoded home paths or deprecated flags. See `README.md` in this dir for the canonical workflow.

## Why this matters

Without migration, your repo's tests/demos are running against the deprecated `cor24-run` binary in `work/bin/`. That binary is slated for retirement once all consumers migrate. The longer your repo waits, the more likely it'll silently break when mike removes `cor24-run`. Migrate now, ship a clean repo, future-proof yourself.

content.text:
Architectural answer: **no new brief needed**. `dc-migrate-toolchain.md` is exactly the brief — it's generic, applies to any `dc*` agent still using `cor24-run --run`, and the mapping is definitive. Pre-flight in this repo: 64 demo runners + `run-subset-tests.sh` + `run-chibicc-test.sh` all use `cor24-run --run`. dcxtc is squarely in scope.

**Where the fix lands:** in *this* repo. Migrate every `cor24-run --run x.s [opts]` → `cor24-asm x.s -o /tmp/x.lgo && cor24-emu --lgo /tmp/x.lgo [opts]`. The dcemu side is already done — `cor24-emu` exists on PATH (May 8) and only takes `.lgo`/`--load-binary`, no internal assembler. The brief explicitly notes that `dcemu`'s `pr/remove-internal-assembler` saga shipped, and `cor24-run` is "slated for retirement once all consumers migrate."

**What it takes to phase out cor24-rs:**
1. Each consumer migrates per `dc-migrate-toolchain.md` (this repo: a new saga `pr/migrate-toolchain`).
2. Once all consumers migrate, mike retires `cor24-run` from `work/bin/`.
3. `cor24-rs.git` becomes archive material.

**This unblocks `pr/emit-zero-fill`.** After migrate-toolchain ships in this repo:
- Demos run via `cor24-emu --lgo` (no internal assembler).
- `cor24-asm` is the only assembler in the pipeline, and it handles `.zero N` correctly (already verified — the round-trip was byte-identical at the cor24-asm level).
- The 5-line `.zero N` change is then trivially correct end-to-end.

So the right sequence is:

| Order | Saga | Why |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `pr/migrate-toolchain` (apply existing dc brief to this repo) | Architecturally clean, unblocks #2, applies generic brief that's been waiting for dcxtc to act on |
| 2 | `pr/emit-zero-fill` (re-do the 5-line change + tests) | Trivially correct once #1 lands |

For the **current state of `pr/emit-zero-fill`** (in-progress, blocked):
- `data.rs` reverted to working loop (no source change).
- `codegen_checks.rs` still has 6 modified-but-uncommitted zero-fill tests.
- agentrail step `015-emit-zero-fill` status `in-progress`, only the saga-insertion commit exists.

Cleanest path forward: **revert the test changes**, **`agentrail abort` the emit-zero-fill step** (marks it blocked), then **insert and start `pr/migrate-toolchain` as the new step 015**. Re-insert emit-zero-fill after migrate-toolchain lands.

Confirm and I'll proceed; or redirect.

message.content: y

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
ROOT_DIR="$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")"
CC24="$ROOT_DIR/components/cli/target/release/tc24r"
DEMO_C="$SCRIPT_DIR/demo15.c"
DEMO_S="$SCRIPT_DIR/demo15.s"
if [ ! -f "$CC24" ]; then cargo build --manifest-path "$ROOT_DIR/components/cli/Cargo.toml" --release --quiet; fi

echo "=== tc24r Demo 15: Ternary Operator ==="
"$CC24" "$DEMO_C" -o "$DEMO_S"
OUTPUT=$(cor24-run --run "$DEMO_S" --dump --speed 0 --time 10 2>&1)
echo "$OUTPUT"
echo ""
R0=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | grep "r0:" | head -1 | awk -F'[()]' '{print $2}' | tr -d ' ')
HALTED=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | grep "Halted:" | head -1 | awk '{print $2}')
UART=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | grep "UART TX log:" | awk -F'UART TX log:' '{print $2}' | tr -d ' "')
echo "=== Validation ==="
PASS=true
if [ "$HALTED" = "true" ]; then echo "  [PASS] CPU halted"; else echo "  [FAIL] no halt"; PASS=false; fi
if [ "$R0" = "42" ]; then echo "  [PASS] r0 = 42"; else echo "  [FAIL] r0 = $R0"; PASS=false; fi
if echo "$UART" | grep -q "D15OK"; then echo "  [PASS] UART: D15OK"; else echo "  [FAIL] UART: '$UART'"; PASS=false; fi
echo ""
if [ "$PASS" = true ]; then echo "Demo 15 PASSED"; else echo "Demo 15 FAILED"; exit 1; fi
---
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
ROOT_DIR="$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")"
CC24="$ROOT_DIR/components/cli/target/release/tc24r"
INCLUDE_DIR="$ROOT_DIR/include"
SRC="$SCRIPT_DIR/demo55.c"
cargo build --manifest-path "$ROOT_DIR/components/cli/Cargo.toml" --release --quiet
TMPDIR=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$TMPDIR"' EXIT
"$CC24" "$SRC" -o "$TMPDIR/demo55.s" -I "$INCLUDE_DIR"
echo "=== Compiled demo55.c ==="
OUTPUT=$(cor24-run --run "$TMPDIR/demo55.s" --dump --speed 0 --time 10 2>&1)
R0=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | grep "r0:" | head -1 | awk -F'[()]' '{print $2}' | tr -d ' ')
HALTED=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | grep "Halted:" | head -1 | awk '{print $2}')
if [ "$HALTED" = "true" ] && [ "$R0" = "55" ]; then
    echo "Demo 55 PASSED (r0=55)"
else
    echo "Demo 55 FAILED (r0=$R0, halted=$HALTED)"
    exit 1
fi

content.text: Two main patterns. Writing a one-shot migration helper to apply the substitution uniformly.

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""One-shot helper to migrate demo runners + test scripts off cor24-run.

Substitutes:
    OUTPUT=$(cor24-run --run "$X" [opts] 2>&1)
With:
    cor24-asm "$X" -o "<LGO>"
    OUTPUT=$(cor24-emu --lgo "<LGO>" [opts] 2>&1)

Where <LGO> is "$TMPDIR/<basename>.lgo" if TMPDIR exists in the file
(new-style runners), else a freshly created tempfile (added via
mktemp + trap) on old-style runners.
"""

import re
import sys
from pathlib import Path

# Match the cor24-run --run invocation (with optional opts after).
RUN_RE = re.compile(
    r'^(\s*OUTPUT=\$\()cor24-run --run "([^"]+)"\s+(--[^)]+)\)$',
    re.MULTILINE,
)

# Detect existing TMPDIR setup.
HAS_TMPDIR_RE = re.compile(r'TMPDIR=\$\(mktemp\s+-d\)', re.MULTILINE)


def derive_lgo_path(s_path: str) -> tuple[str, bool]:
    """Return (lgo_path, needs_local_tmp).

    needs_local_tmp = True if we need a one-shot mktemp inside the
    inserted block (old-style runners writing .s to demos/).
    """
    if s_path.startswith("$TMPDIR/"):
        # New-style: put .lgo next to .s in TMPDIR.
        return s_path.removesuffix(".s") + ".lgo", False
    # Old-style: .s is in demos/ — write .lgo to a tempfile.
    return None, True  # caller fills in


def migrate_text(text: str) -> str:
    has_tmpdir = bool(HAS_TMPDIR_RE.search(text))

    def repl(m):
        prefix = m.group(1)            # "OUTPUT=$("
        s_path = m.group(2)            # "$DEMO_S" or "$TMPDIR/demoN.s"
        opts = m.group(3).strip()      # "--dump --speed 0 --time 10 2>&1"
        # Drop the trailing ` 2>&1` from opts; we'll re-emit it.
        opts_clean = re.sub(r'\s*2>&1\s*$', '', opts)

        lgo_path, needs_local_tmp = derive_lgo_path(s_path)

        if needs_local_tmp:
            # Old-style: introduce a tempfile for the .lgo. Use a
            # short variable so it's obvious this is the new pipeline.
            asm_block = (
                'LGO=$(mktemp --suffix=.lgo)\n'
                f'        cor24-asm "{s_path}" -o "$LGO"\n'
                f'        {prefix}cor24-emu --lgo "$LGO" {opts_clean} 2>&1)\n'
                '        rm -f "$LGO"'
            )
            # Strip the leading 8 spaces we sprinkled (they're for inside
            # an indented block; outer caller fixes top-level indent).
            asm_block = '\n'.join(line[8:] if line.startswith('        ') else line
                                  for line in asm_block.split('\n'))
            return asm_block
        else:
            # New-style: lgo lives in TMPDIR alongside the .s.
            return (
                f'cor24-asm "{s_path}" -o "{lgo_path}"\n'
                f'{prefix}cor24-emu --lgo "{lgo_path}" {opts_clean} 2>&1)'
            )

    return RUN_RE.sub(repl, text)


def main(argv):
    if len(argv) < 2:
        print("usage: migrate.py <file>...", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2
    changed = 0
    skipped = 0
    for path_str in argv[1:]:
        p = Path(path_str)
        text = p.read_text()
        new_text = migrate_text(text)
        if new_text == text:
            skipped += 1
            continue
        p.write_text(new_text)
        changed += 1
    print(f"changed {changed}, unchanged {skipped}")
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main(sys.argv))

file.content:
compile_fail=0
total=0

for f in "$SUBSET_DIR"/*.c; do
    name=$(basename "$f" .c)
    total=$((total + 1))
    TMPDIR=$(mktemp -d)

    if "$CC24" "$f" -o "$TMPDIR/$name.s" -I "$INCLUDE_DIR" 2>"$TMPDIR/err"; then
        result=$(cor24-run --run "$TMPDIR/$name.s" --dump --speed 0 --time 10 2>&1)
        r0=$(echo "$result" | grep "r0:" | head -1 | awk -F'[()]' '{print $2}' | tr -d ' ')
        halted=$(echo "$result" | grep "Halted:" | head -1 | awk '{print $2}')

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Run all chibicc-subset tests through tc24r -> cor24-run pipeline.
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
ROOT_DIR="$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")"
CC24="$ROOT_DIR/components/cli/target/release/tc24r"
INCLUDE_DIR="$ROOT_DIR/include"
SUBSET_DIR="$ROOT_DIR/tests/chibicc-subset"

cargo build --manifest-path "$ROOT_DIR/components/cli/Cargo.toml" --release --quiet

pass=0
fail=0
compile_fail=0
total=0

for f in "$SUBSET_DIR"/*.c; do
    name=$(basename "$f" .c)
    total=$((total + 1))
    TMPDIR=$(mktemp -d)

    if "$CC24" "$f" -o "$TMPDIR/$name.s" -I "$INCLUDE_DIR" 2>"$TMPDIR/err"; then
        result=$(cor24-run --run "$TMPDIR/$name.s" --dump --speed 0 --time 10 2>&1)
        r0=$(echo "$result" | grep "r0:" | head -1 | awk -F'[()]' '{print $2}' | tr -d ' ')
        halted=$(echo "$result" | grep "Halted:" | head -1 | awk '{print $2}')
        if [ "$halted" = "true" ] && [ "$r0" = "0" ]; then
            echo "  PASS: $name"
            pass=$((pass + 1))
        elif [ "$halted" = "true" ]; then
            echo "  FAIL: $name (r0=$r0)"
            fail=$((fail + 1))
        else
            echo "  TIMEOUT: $name"
            fail=$((fail + 1))
        fi
    else
        echo "  COMPILE_FAIL: $name -- $(head -1 "$TMPDIR/err")"
        compile_fail=$((compile_fail + 1))
    fi
    rm -rf "$TMPDIR"
done

echo ""
echo "=== chibicc-subset test results ==="
echo "  Pass:         $pass / $total"
echo "  Fail:         $fail / $total"
echo "  Compile fail: $compile_fail / $total"
pct=$((pass * 100 / total))
echo "  Coverage: ${pct}%"

git add demos/ scripts/ .agentrail/ && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
build(scripts): migrate demo runners + test scripts off cor24-run

Apply dc-migrate-toolchain.md to this repo. Every cor24-run --run call
site is replaced with the canonical cor24-asm + cor24-emu --lgo pair:

    OLD:  OUTPUT=$(cor24-run --run "$X" [opts] 2>&1)
    NEW:  cor24-asm "$X" -o "$LGO"
          OUTPUT=$(cor24-emu --lgo "$LGO" [opts] 2>&1)

Sites migrated (66 total):
  - 64 demo runners under demos/ (+ unnumbered demos/run-demo.sh)
  - scripts/run-subset-tests.sh
  - scripts/run-chibicc-test.sh

For old-style runners (writing .s to demos/<demo>.s) a one-shot
mktemp .lgo file is created and cleaned up around the run. New-style
runners (TMPDIR-based) put the .lgo next to the .s in the same TMPDIR.

Why now: dcemu's pr/remove-internal-assembler shipped (cor24-emu has
no internal assembler; only --lgo / --load-binary / --demo). cor24-run
is slated for retirement once all consumers migrate. dc-migrate-
toolchain.md has been waiting for dcxtc to act on it.

Specifically unblocks pr/emit-zero-fill: cor24-run's internal assembler
silently mishandles cor24-asm's .zero N directive (accepts the syntax
but emits the wrong number of bytes — verified during the aborted
emit-zero-fill attempt). cor24-asm + cor24-emu has no such issue —
.zero N round-trips byte-identical .lgo. Once this lands, the .zero N
emission saga can be re-inserted and shipped trivially.

Verification:
  - 60 / 64 demos pass post-migration
  - 4 pre-existing failures (demo2, demo8, demo10 — flagged by
    dcxtc-demo-tests-investigate-negative.md as exit_code=1 negative

  - demos/*.s codegen output unchanged (restored after test runs)

Migration script (one-shot, not committed): /tmp/asx-repro/migrate.py.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
